input:
Re-hospitalization form:
This is the child AZZEMMOU MAHDI born on 04/23/2016, currently aged 9 years and 7 months, originally from and living in MOSTAGANEM, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed at the EPH BOUGUIRAT MOSTAGANEM since 2022 for a primary immunodeficiency such as congenital agammaglobulinemia, hospitalized at our level today for the immunoglobulin treatment N: 40. Main diagnosis: Primary immunodeficiency type Congenital Agammaglobulinemia
Associated diagnoses: None
Date of diagnosis: 08/23/2022. Age of diagnosis: 6 years and 4 months
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics:
-A brother died in 2022 in respiratory distress at the Canastel pediatric intensive care unit (IMC due to meningitis at the age of 6)
- A maternal uncle died at the age of 18 months from unknown etiologies. Clinic:
-Recurrent purulent ear infections with arthritis of the left knee since the age of 5. -Chronic wet cough since the age of 5. Paraclinic:
-Immune deficiency assessment: done on: 08/23/2022:
-IgG: 0.33 g/l IgA: 0.26 g/l IgM: 0.181 g/l. -Absence of B lymphocytes. The picture is in favor of congenital Agammaglobulinemia. NB: need to repeat the immune assessment for the child and for the parents (mother). Processing in progress:
-Itraconazole 1 tab/day
-Bactrim 1 tab day a day
-Physiotherapy
Repercussions of the illness:
Nutritionally:
Weight: 28,800kg (+0.55DS). Height: 132 cm (+0.43 SD) BMI: 16.53%
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
On the respiratory level:
-6 min walking test: done without abnormalities. To be completed with spirometry
-Front chest x-ray: bilateral infiltrates in both pulmonary fields predominating in bilateral para hilar. -ECB and sputum parasitology: culture: polymorphic flora of commensal type. -Physiotherapy with therapeutic education. -Thoracic CT: 09/15/2025: bilateral parenchymal condensation involving the middle lobe and heterogeneous lingula by the presence of air bronchogram: aspect of multifocal bronchopneumopathy. On the cardiac level:
-Echocoeur: 09/17/2025: without anomalies. On the ENT level:
-An ENT opinion made for recurrent ear infections: objectivizing:
Bilateral tympanic retraction
Intermediate history: Since the last treatment: 11/24/2025
- Consultation: 0
- Appetite preserved
- On the respiratory level: cough with greenish sputum evolving in an afebrile context for a week, without signs of respiratory distress,
-On the eye level: redness in both eyes with purulent morning secretion (conjunctivitis?) treated by the mother with eye drops (not brought back by the mother)
Biological assessment
- Residual IgG dosage: (10/18/2025) 14.9 g/dl
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: 12/22/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 28,500 kg (-0.5DS)., Height: 134 cm (-0.3 DS) BMI: 15.9 (-0.7DS)
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 37.1C, good mucocutaneous coloring. Good state of hydration. Examination of the skin and appendages:
No scalp lesions. Presence of scars from traumatic injuries on the left knee. Presence of small brownish hyperpigmentation lesions on the face. Head and neck examination:
No particular features, no craniofacial dysmorphia. Normally implanted ears. No cervical ADP. ENT examination: Absence of otorrhea, absence of ear pain. Clean throat, tooth decay
ENT opinion made: Bilateral tympanic retraction
Ophthalmic examination: Conjunctival hyperemia
Pleuropulmonary examination: Wet cough, slightly greenish sputum
No chest deformation, no sign of chronic respiratory failure. FR: 25 cycles/min, SPO2: 99% under ambient air,
Pulmonary auscultation: polymorphic rales of bronchial congestion in both pulmonary fields. Cardiovascular examination: HR. : 80 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 clearly audible, no murmurs or added noises. The rest of the exam: Is unremarkable. Summary of 12/22/2025:
FNS: Coagulated blood
CRP: <6mg/l negative
PCT: 0.05ng/ml
HDJ support; Replacement immunotherapy at a rate of 0.9g/kg or 25g, brand: Intratect passed without incident
DISCHARGE Discharged the same day, after the end of the treatment. Prophylactic treatment Bactrim 30mg/kg/day curative dose with Augmentin 150mg/kg/day distributed every 8 hours.
Antifungal prophylaxis itraconazole orally 5mg/kg/day
Hygiene and diet advice
ETP and monitoring notebooks
Plan courses of multivalent immunoglobulin infusions every month. Control appointment at the CS DIP of Professor Drali
Reports requested:
Residual IgG dosage every 3 months
Referral letter to ophthalmologist. Review ENT control
Schedule ECBC with control appointment (after retrieval of results)
Plan hearing assessment
Provide spirometry
Dermatological opinion
Stomatology advice
Recheck chest CT
Recover the assessment of the mother's immune deficiency
Predict aspergillus serology
CONCLUSION:
The child AZZEMMOU MAHDI aged 9 years and 7 months, originally from and residing in Mostaganem, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed for a DIP type agammaglobulinemia, Admitted to our level for his treatment of immunoglobulin No. 40. History of hospitalizations:
